steps.title: Test the universality claim
steps.content: Vargas and colleagues challenge the idea that cultures universally distinguish time along imagined linear axes. The strongest support would show that Yupno speakers do not use opposite linear directions to separate past-oriented and future-oriented gestures.

steps.title: Ignore irrelevant gesture facts
steps.content: Choice A concerns which hand speakers use, not whether gestures oppose each other along an axis. Choice C compares grammar with Hebrew, not gestures. Choice D concerns indoor and outdoor frequency. The remaining choice directly undermines the proposed universal pattern. The answer is B.
